description:
You are acting as a junior security-auditor who has been asked to take a quick snapshot of the system’s current network exposure.  
Your deliverable is a plain-text log file that follows the exact structure described below so that an automated test script can parse it verbatim.

Target log file (must be newly created):
        /home/user/audit/netcheck.log

Required directory:
        /home/user/audit                   (create it if it does not already exist)

File permissions:
        • netcheck.log must end up world-readable, group-readable and user-writable only (octal 0644).

Data that must appear in the log file, in the precise order shown:

1. The literal header line (no leading/trailing spaces):
        INTERFACE SUMMARY

2. One line for every interface reported by the command
        ip -o -4 addr show
   Each line must contain exactly:
        <interface_name>:<IPv4/CIDR>
   Example format (actual values will vary):
        eth0:192.168.1.10/24
   Rules:
        • Only IPv4 addresses (use the “-4” flag as above).
        • Sort the lines in **lexicographical order** by interface_name.
        • Do NOT include loopback interfaces whose name equals “lo”.

3. A single blank line.

4. The literal header line:
        OPEN TCP PORTS

5. One line for every port number currently in LISTEN state for IPv4 TCP sockets, as reported by
        ss -lnt4
   Each line must contain exactly:
        PORT=<port_number>
   Example format:
        PORT=22
   Rules:
        • Extract the port number from the Local Address:Port field.
        • List each distinct port only once, even if it is bound on multiple addresses.
        • Sort the lines in **ascending numeric order**.

Important notes / hints to the agent (these are requirements, not suggestions):
• Do NOT include any extra commentary in the log file—only the lines described above.
• The headers “INTERFACE SUMMARY” and “OPEN TCP PORTS” must be present exactly as written, in UPPER CASE.
• Lines must be terminated by a single UNIX newline character (“\n”).
• Create /home/user/audit if it does not already exist, without elevated privileges.
• After creating the file, adjust its permissions to 0644.
• Use standard GNU/Linux command-line tools only; no third-party utilities are installed.
• Double-check that the resulting /home/user/audit/netcheck.log matches the required format before finishing.

The automated grader will open /home/user/audit/netcheck.log and compare its contents and its file mode against these specifications. Make sure everything matches precisely.
truth:
The test harness will:

1. Verify that the file /home/user/audit/netcheck.log exists and is readable.
2. Confirm its mode is exactly 100644 (rw-r--r--).
3. Re-generate two reference lists inside the running container:
        a) Reference interface list produced by:
                ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$2!="lo"{print $2":"$4}' | sort
        b) Reference port list produced by:
                ss -lnt4H | awk '{split($4,a,":");print a[length(a)]}' | sort -n | uniq | awk '{print "PORT="$1}'
4. Build the expected file content:
        "INTERFACE SUMMARY\n"
        + reference interface list (already newline-terminated)
        + "\n"
        + "OPEN TCP PORTS\n"
        + reference port list (newline-terminated, may be empty)
5. Compare the grader-generated string byte-for-byte with the contents of /home/user/audit/netcheck.log.

A perfect match is required for the task to pass.
name: task_000000_e09f6b90
